perturbation.original_text: At the written request of the University or upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return or destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: At the written request of the University, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return the Information. Reproduction restrictions are waived.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause regarding the destruction of information upon expiry or termination and the restriction on retaining photocopies, the modification creates an inconsistency. The original clause imposes a strict requirement to either return or destroy the information and prohibits retention. By only requiring the information to be returned, and stating that 'Reproduction restrictions are waived' it now contradicts the original intention and makes it ambigious if the Recipient is suppose to destroy it, now that the reproduction restriction is waived. The absence of both create uncertainty around the full extent of the recipient's obligations with regards to the information's destruction, which previously was required
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return or Destruction of Information. At the written request of the University, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return the Information. Reproduction restrictions are waived.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the destruction requirement and states 'Reproduction restrictions are waived', while the contradicted text has the clause 'Reproduction restrictions are waived.' The removal of 'or destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information' from the original text introduces a contradiction. The contradictory text omits the destruction element which was a part of the original text and now only contains the Reproduction restrictions are waived.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the core understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause is an entire agreement clause, explicitly stating that this document contains the entire agreement and understanding, superseding all prior agreements and negotiations. The changed text omits the 'entire agreement' portion, only stating that the agreement contains the 'core understanding'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it suggests that other agreements or understandings outside the written document might still be valid and applicable, thus contradicting the original intent that this document stands alone.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement and Counterpart. This Agreement contains the core understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter. This Agreement may be signed in counterparts and faxed to the other party or parties, and each counterpart, together with the other counterparts will constitute the entire Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained an entire agreement clause, stating that the document supersedes all prior agreements. The changed text removes this, stating only that the agreement contains the 'core understanding'. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' section because it opens the possibility that other agreements outside this written document are valid, conflicting with the 'Entire Agreement' clause still present in the contradicted section. The contradicted section also uses 'core understanding', but it does that as one of many parts of the entire agreement and understanding, which differs from the changed text, which only agrees to the 'core understanding'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construed under the laws of British Columbia and the applicable laws of Canada without reference to its conflict of law rules.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by the laws of Canada.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that the agreement should be governed by the laws of British Columbia AND the laws of Canada. The change omits reference to laws in British Columbia. Given that both the university and recipient have offices in British Columbia, the omission introduces ambiguity and could create a dispute about which jurisdiction's laws should prevail for certain matters. It contradicts the intent of having both
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law and Jurisdiction. The Agreement will be governed by the laws of Canada. Any action or proceeding brought to enforce the terms of this Agreement will be brought in a court in Vancouver, British Columbia, and the parties hereby consent and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of such court.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the agreement would be governed by both British Columbia and Canadian law. The changed text omits British Columbia law. The contradicted text states that the agreement will be governed by the laws of Canada and that any legal action will be brought in a court in British Columbia. The omission of British Columbia law in the changed text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, as it introduces ambiguity about the governing law and the jurisdiction, especially because the contradicted text explicitly mentions that proceedings to enforce terms would happen in British Columbia. Thus, under Omissions – In Text Contradiction, this is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not use the Information for any purpose other than to evaluate the Information for commercial potential. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Recipient will not use the Information to develop, or cause to develop, all or part of any process or product whether for internal use or for commercial purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will use the Information only to evaluate the Information for commercial potential.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly prohibits using the information to 'develop or cause to develop' any process or product, whether for internal or commercial purposes. The changed text omits this prohibition. The omission creates ambiguity because it no longer explicitly prevents the recipient from using the Information to develop a process or product, as long as it's related to *evaluating* commercial potential. This contradicts the stricter terms of the original agreement and the intent of stopping the recipient to developing, either for internal or external use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use. The Recipient will use the Information only to evaluate the Information for commercial potential. The Recipient hereby indemnifies, holds harmless and defends the University, its Board of Governors, directors, officers, employees, faculty, students and agents against any and all claims, demands, liabilities and expenses (including reasonable legal fees and disbursements), whether direct, indirect, consequential or otherwise, resulting from a breach of this provision or any other provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits the Recipient from using the Information to 'develop or cause to develop' any process or product, for any purpose. The changed text removes this explicit prohibition, stating only that the Information will be used 'to evaluate the Information for commercial potential.' This creates ambiguity and implicitly allows development activities as long as they are related to the evaluation of commercial potential. The Contradicted Text states, 'The Recipient will use the Information only to evaluate the Information for commercial potential,' and includes an indemnification clause for breaches. However, the omission in the changed text weakens the protection afforded to the University since the scope of prohibited activities is narrowed, and it is now unclear if using the Information to develop a product constitutes a breach since it might be seen as related to 'evaluating' the Information.
